details_md:
1. files changed

- [src/styles/custom.css](/Users/ai-paramita/Desktop/Project/Paramita-Loom/src/styles/custom.css)
- [src/components/GuideMetadata.astro](/Users/ai-paramita/Desktop/Project/Paramita-Loom/src/components/GuideMetadata.astro)
- [src/content/docs/index.mdx](/Users/ai-paramita/Desktop/Project/Paramita-Loom/src/content/docs/index.mdx)

2. what was done

- Added `--loom-radius-sm/md/lg`, replaced the specified card/container radii with tokens, moved `.loom-card` and `.loom-review-box` to `var(--loom-card)`, removed the `.loom-section::before` wash overlay, raised the light-mode `--loom-soft` contrast color, and added the `72rem` tablet breakpoint in [src/styles/custom.css](/Users/ai-paramita/Desktop/Project/Paramita-Loom/src/styles/custom.css).
- Converted GuideMetadata secondary sections to native collapsed `<details>` / `<summary>` progressive disclosure while keeping primary rows, projection, and tags always visible in [src/components/GuideMetadata.astro](/Users/ai-paramita/Desktop/Project/Paramita-Loom/src/components/GuideMetadata.astro).
- Removed the duplicate English landing-page “Reading order” card grid and folded the reading-order guidance into the approved-batch intro in [src/content/docs/index.mdx](/Users/ai-paramita/Desktop/Project/Paramita-Loom/src/content/docs/index.mdx).

3. why this is minimal

- The change stays inside the three files that actually needed behavior or styling updates.
- `ReviewBox.astro` did not require markup changes because its class hook already existed; the requested ReviewBox unification was satisfied centrally in CSS.
- No content schema, routing, component structure outside `GuideMetadata`, or zh-hk landing page content was changed.

4. validation performed

- Ran `npm run build` successfully.
- Build warnings observed but unchanged from this task:
  - Astro/Vite unused import warning from dependency code.
  - Existing `/404` route conflict warning during static generation.
- I also attempted to create the requested rollback git tag `pre-s1-679737890abd020b`, but the sandbox blocked writes to `.git/refs/tags`, so that tag was not created.

5. open risks

- I could not do browser-based visual regression, dark-mode inspection, or manual responsive checks for mobile/tablet/desktop in this CLI-only turn.
- Native `<details>` styling now depends on Starlight not adding conflicting summary styles; the build passed, but this still needs visual confirmation.
- The tablet rule forces `.loom-guide-metadata-primary` to 3 columns up to `72rem`; this matches the requirement, but should be visually checked around the breakpoint.

6. recommended next step

- Run the site in dev and verify three views manually: English landing page, a guide page with `GuideMetadata`, and a page with `ReviewBox`, checking light/dark mode and the `<50rem`, `50-72rem`, `>72rem` ranges.
